channel.title: Random Ideas on Utsab's Digital Garden
channel.description: Recent content in Random Ideas on Utsab's Digital Garden

item.title: Any other ways than deterministic and Probabilistic Modelling
item.description: #to_ponder #ideas While the deterministic and probabilistic paradigms dominate modern machine learning, several other frameworks exist that model uncertainty and logic in fundamentally different ways. These paradigms are often used when data is scarce, contradictory, or when researchers try to capture the “non-rational” nuances of human behavior. Alternative Modeling Paradigms in ML Beyond the standard deterministic $f(x) = y$ and probabilistic $P(y|x)$ approaches, several specialized theories provide different mathematical structures for handling information:

item.title: Can we use JEPA for Games??
item.description: #to_ponder World Models for games? Since the goal of a world model is to understand the consequences of its actions would it lead to better players?

item.title: Can you project tasks to similar Task-spaces?
item.description: #to_ponder #continual_learning Catastrophic Forgetting happens mostly when tasks are moderately to highly dissimilar to the previous tasks. (depending on the parameterisation level - over or critically parameterised) Can we somehow fake the next set of tasks to be similar to the previous tasks. Like say some transformation on task 2 to project it into task 1 ’s distribution or smth and then putting an inverse transformation to bring it back to the task-2 space?

item.title: Capacity of a Model
item.description: #to_ponder To learn information, to hold information The Gap You’re Pointing At What doesn’t exist is a per-model, per-task characterization of the generalization ceiling — something like: “a transformer of this size, trained optimally, can generalize to functions of at most this complexity, this frequency content, with this many tasks.” The pieces are there — information-theoretic bounds, spectral bias analysis, Kolmogorov complexity arguments, scaling law empirics — but they haven’t been assembled into a coherent framework that gives you the equivalent of Shannon capacity for a specific architecture on a specific task class.

item.title: Encoding Future Related Hope as a Learning Signal?
Manifolds of Data vs Implicit Curricula
New way of training small models?
item.description: #to_ponder What if you allot different parts of the parameter space to learning kinds of data in a somewhat explicit manner? Then we use a smaller extractor layer to essentially only make use of the parameters of the required subspace for processing the given input to get the required output. The architecture might be : A big network to store the representations in kinda well defined sub-param spaces for the input data distribution A smaller network which decides which subspace to extract the information from conditioned on the given input.

item.title: Teach a model to solve a patch jigsaw?
Why do Diffusion Models Generalise So Well?
item.description: #to_ponder #diffusion #compvis Can we somehow use properties of this to improve OOD Generalisation in other non-genrative vision models? It seems the iterative nature of these models lead to learning of the low level features for better generalisation. If we design the non-generative framework in a similar iterative manner would that lead to OOD Gen?? Models can do two things depending on the regime(needs to be studied): Gen -> overfit Overfit -> gen (grokking) Geminis’ version on Gen vs Non-Gen Vis Generalisation Ability!

item.title: Why do we need large embedding sizes?
